title: The Radio Talk in Ep 20
wikitext:
[]: The Radio Talk in Ep. 20             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Feb 23, 2007 4:48 am The episode where Shinji is taken into Yui via Shogouki. Gotta love the "random" radio chatter in the car as Misato and Ritsuko are driving home: Ep.20 wrote: RADIO: Sure, I can understand, but I guess that's what's called the oral stage. RADIO: It's something a psychologist came up with a long time ago. RADIO: In other words, it's where you want to be with your mother forever. It refers to people who want to always be dependent on someone. There's someone like that among my acquaintences too, and you're a lot like him. RADIO: So, well, as far as I can see from your letter, from your girlfriend's standpoint, RADIO: being your lover, your mother, and what else, your kid sister? RADIO: I think being all of these things for you is pretty rough. And you know, aren't you sort of taking advantage of her place as a lover, RADIO: using it as your personal outlet for your libido? Okay, well, that might have been a little extreme. RADIO: But a woman is very sensitive to whether someone loves her or not. So I think she's probably twigged onto that by now, that you're looking for a mother you can sleep with in her. RADIO: And if she still hasn't brought up splitting up with you, RADIO: she might be one of those nice girls that are pretty rare these days, you know?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 23, 2007 12:31 pm Always pay attention to the background noise/info in Eva. Usually it ends up being important. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Houndour » Fri Feb 23, 2007 12:33 pm Ties in with the episode's name, too. Stuff in the background always means something in NGE. Normally I can't read the subtitles for radios (or televisions, for that matter) in time without completely missing the main conversation, so I usually have to watch scenes like this a couple times.

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Fri Feb 23, 2007 12:49 pm My favorite background dialogue was from Ep. 4, when Shinji goes to the movie theater; one Ep. 4 translation wrote: Man A (Movie): Could you really detect that? Doctor (Movie): Yes. An object with a diameter of centimeters impacted Antarctica at more than ten percent of the speed of light. Man A (Movie): With our current scientific knowledge, we could neither detect it nor prevent it. Woman (Movie): It's hell on Earth outside. What does science exist for?! Man B (Movie): At present, the atmospheric flow caused by the transition of the Earth's axis has decreased. Woman (Movie): Has it calmed down a little? Man B (Movie): No. A tidal wave is approaching with a velocity of 230 meters per second. Man A (Movie): Doctor, we should escape. Doctor (Movie): It's my duty to stay here. Man A (Movie): Doctor, it's easy to die. However, you have an obligation to care for this hellish world...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 23, 2007 3:04 pm LOL!!! Irony, irony is good. ^^; Shinji's such a prude! At that point, anyway... The dub really hams that scene up, though I obviously perfer the subs.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Feb 23, 2007 7:24 pm The episode #20 dialogue is most probably referring to Shinji's own reluctance to "leave" his mother. However there may be a more morbid undertone in the lines concerning dependancy and "a mother you can sleep with", in relation to Misato's own subconscious designs for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 24, 2007 3:41 am If it's about people, any leak of broadcast media is sly commentary on events (as opposed to scene setting, like the early radio news and weather report about a bombing, and hot weather) For example I've not figured out yet how the mathematical puppy fits into the model -- it would seem rather cruel as the commentary on a pilot's role.

[]: Re: The Radio Talk in Ep. 20             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Feb 24, 2007 6:48 am I have actually just done this in psychology. Further details: RADIO: Sure, I can understand, but I guess that's what's called the oral stage.

[]: Re: The Radio Talk in Ep. 20             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Feb 24, 2007 2:10 pm Szmitten wrote: I have actually just done this in psychology. Not quite. This stuff is way off.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 24, 2007 2:31 pm RADIO: But a woman is very sensitive to whether someone loves her or not. So I think she's probably twigged onto that by now, that you're looking for a mother you can sleep with in her.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Feb 24, 2007 2:57 pm Reichu wrote: For some reason, this made me think of the actual events precipitating in the car, considering Misato is driving off to see her designated Daddy I Can Sleep With.

[]: Re: The Radio Talk in Ep. 20             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Feb 24, 2007 4:34 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Szmitten wrote: I have actually just done this in psychology. Not quite. This stuff is way off.

[]: Re: The Radio Talk in Ep. 20             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 24, 2007 5:15 pm Szmitten wrote: Phallic involves boys suddenly realising that they have a penis, and this is where the Oedipus Complex happens. For girls, they supposedly become envious and blame their mother that they don't have one.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 26, 2007 3:50 pm Yeah, some of the things Freud said were out there... -_-
